question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getRegisteredNodes(RegisteredNodesRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user supply `registeredNodeId=eq:X` combined with another `registeredNodeId` parameter to trigger the `IllegalArgumentException('The eq operator cannot be combined with other operators')` check in `resolveRegisteredNodeIdBounds()`, and by doing so repeatedly, cause excessive exception creation and stack trace generation that degrades JVM performance?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getRegisteredNodes(RegisteredNodesRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user supply `registeredNodeId=gt:9223372036854775806` to cause `NumberRangeParameter.getInclusiveValue()` to return `Long.MIN_VALUE` (overflow), setting `lowerBound = Long.MIN_VALUE` via `Math.max(0, Long.MIN_VALUE) = 0`, effectively querying from node ID 0 to `Long.MAX_VALUE` and returning all registered nodes regardless of the intended filter?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getRegisteredNodes(RegisteredNodesRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user supply `registeredNodeId=lt:1` to cause `getInclusiveValue()` to return `0`, and since `lowerBound` defaults to `0`, the resulting `Range.closed(0, 0)` queries only node ID 0, which may not exist, returning an empty list that misrepresents the registered node set to consumers relying on this data for network topology decisions?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getRegisteredNodes(RegisteredNodesRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `@Size(max = 2)` constraint on `registeredNodeIds` being enforced only at the DTO validation layer, and if validation is bypassed (e.g., via direct service invocation or validation misconfiguration), submit a list with many range parameters to cause `resolveRegisteredNodeIdBounds()` to iterate over all of them, consuming excessive CPU in the loop?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getRegisteredNodes(RegisteredNodesRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user supply `type=MIRROR_NODE` combined with a wide node ID range to cause `findByRegisteredNodeIdBetweenAndDeletedIsFalseAndTypeIs()` to perform a full table scan filtered only by type, and with `limit=MAX_LIMIT` set to a large value, retrieve the complete list of mirror nodes in the network, enabling targeted attacks against specific mirror node infrastructure?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: resolveRegisteredNodeIdBounds(List<NumberRangeParameter>)] Can an unprivileged external user supply `registeredNodeId=gte:0&registeredNodeId=lte:9223372036854775807` to set `lowerBound=0` and `upperBound=Long.MAX_VALUE`, causing `findByRegisteredNodeIdBetweenAndDeletedIsFalseAndTypeIs(0, Long.MAX_VALUE, null, page)` to perform a full table scan of all registered nodes, limited only by the page size, enabling complete network topology enumeration?
